Carbon & Climate Solutions

From inventory to offset, we provide the technical architecture for your Net Zero journey. We calculate, verify, and mitigate your carbon footprint across all Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions.

Climate Roadmap

What's Included

Technical infrastructure for the Net-Zero transition.

Scope 1, 2, & 3 GHG Emissions Inventory
Net-Zero Strategy & Science-Based Roadmaps
CBAM Readiness & Carbon Tax Advisory
SBTi Target Setting & Validation Support

The climate challenge

Carbon accounting is no longer just about direct emissions.

01

Scope 3 Blindspots

90% of a company's impact often lies in its supply chain. Measuring this accurately requires complex data modeling and supplier engagement.

02

Greenwashing Risk

Stakeholders are aggressively scrutinizing Net Zero claims. Without rigorous, science-based verification, companies face significant reputation damage.

03

Carbon Pricing

New border taxes like CBAM are turning carbon into a direct financial liability. Managing your footprint is now a core fiscal responsibility.

Net Zero Protocol

A technical path to total decarbonization.

Inventory

Targeting

Climate Assets

We deliver a full technical suite for your carbon management.

CBAM Impact Audit
Supply Chain Scorecard

Measurement Standards

Rigorous protocols for high-precision carbon accounting.

Inventory Protocols
  • GHG Protocol Corporate Standard (Revised Edition)
  • ISO 14064-1 Organization-level quantification & reporting
  • PAS 2060 Specification for Carbon Neutrality
  • WRI/WBCSD Scope 3 Calculation Guidance
Regulatory Analysis
  • EU CBAM Transitional phase reporting (Q1 2024+)
  • SEC Climate Rule Scope 1 & 2 disclosure requirements
  • SBTi Corporate 1.5°C near-term and Net-Zero targets
  • CDP Climate Full disclosure support & scoring optimization

Carbon FAQs

Expert insights on climate strategy and management.

Scope 3 encompasses all indirect emissions that occur in a company's value chain. This includes 15 categories such as purchased goods and services, business travel, waste disposal, and the use of sold products. We map these across your entire supply chain for a complete inventory.
The EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) requires exporters of steel, cement, aluminum, and other carbon-intensive goods to report their embedded emissions. From 2026, financial penalties apply. We provide the "Actual Emissions" auditing required to minimize these costs.
Carbon Neutrality allows for offsetting a large portion of emissions through credits. Net Zero (SBTi aligned) requires significant absolute emission reductions (typically 90%+) across the value chain, with only residual emissions being neutralized through carbon removal projects.
Under the SBTi's Corporate Net-Zero Standard, offsets (avoidance credits) cannot be used to reach near-term or long-term reduction targets. They can only be used for "Beyond Value Chain Mitigation" or to neutralize the final 10% of residual emissions in the Net-Zero target year.
GHG Protocol SBTi Aligned ISO 14064 CBAM Specialist
